# orcasecurity_sensitive_data_identifiers (Data Source)

Lists DSPM sensitive data identifiers (built-in and custom) from GET /api/scan_configuration/dspm_detector. Use it to look up built-in identifier IDs (e.g. `AUS_TAX_NUMBER`) for `orcasecurity_dspm_policy.document.detectors`. Pick an id with a for expression, e.g. one([for i in data.orcasecurity_sensitive_data_identifiers.all.identifiers : i.id if i.title == "Email Address"]).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# look up built-in and custom identifiers in the PII category
data "orcasecurity_sensitive_data_identifiers" "pii" {
  category = "PII"
}

# reference a built-in identifier id by title
locals {
  email_identifier_id = one([
    for identifier in data.orcasecurity_sensitive_data_identifiers.pii.identifiers :
    identifier.id if identifier.title == "Email Address"
  ])
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `category` (String) Filter by category (exact match: `PII`, `PHI`, `PCI`, `SECRET`, `OTHER`).
- `sub_category` (String) Filter by sub-category (exact match).
- `title` (String) Filter by identifier title (exact match).

### Read-Only

- `identifiers` (Attributes List) Each object is one sensitive data identifier from the API. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--identifiers))

<a id="nestedatt--identifiers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `identifiers`

Read-Only:

- `category` (String) Data category.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the identifier is enabled.
- `id` (String) Identifier ID (catalog string for built-ins, UUID for custom identifiers).
- `is_custom` (Boolean) Whether this is a custom (organization-created) identifier.
- `sub_category` (String) Data sub-category.
- `title` (String) Identifier title.
